landmarks.name: Taj Mahal
landmarks.description: Ivory-white marble mausoleum on the Yamuna river
landmarks.significance: Built by Shah Jahan for his wife Mumtaz Mahal. UNESCO World Heritage Site.
didYouKnow: Over 1,000 elephants were used to transport building materials during construction
The Taj Mahal appears to change color - pink at sunrise, white during the day, and golden at sunset
It took 22 years and 20,000 skilled workers to complete this masterpiece
The four minarets lean slightly outward to protect the main tomb in case of an earthquake
Shah Jahan allegedly planned to build a black marble Taj Mahal for himself across the river, but was imprisoned by his son before he could start

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Sunrise (6-7 AM) for fewer crowds, magical light, and cooler temperatures
explorerNotes.entranceFee: ₹1,100 ($13 USD) for foreign tourists. Closed on Fridays. E-tickets available online.
nearbyAttractions: Mehtab Bagh garden - best sunset view (across river)
explorerNotes.travelTip: Buy tickets online to skip 2+ hour queues. No food, smoking, or photography inside the mausoleum. Wear slip-on shoes (you'll remove them often). Avoid full moon nights - extra crowded.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Very hot summers (35-45°C). Pleasant winters (10-25°C). Best months: October to March. Monsoon July-September can be humid.

landmarks.name: Great Pyramid of Giza
landmarks.description: Oldest and largest pyramid in Giza complex
landmarks.significance: Only remaining ancient wonder. Built for Pharaoh Khufu.
didYouKnow: The Great Pyramid was the tallest human-made structure for 3,800 years until Lincoln Cathedral in 1311
It originally stood 146.7m tall but erosion reduced it to 138.8m - still taller than the Statue of Liberty
Over 2.3 million stone blocks were used, each weighing 2.5 to 15 tons
The pyramid's sides are aligned almost perfectly with the four cardinal directions (north, south, east, west)
Despite popular belief, it was NOT built by slaves - archaeologists found evidence of paid workers who received food, shelter, and medical care

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Early morning (8-9 AM) before heat and crowds. October to April for cooler weather.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: EGP 540 ($17 USD) for pyramid complex. Extra EGP 440 ($14) to enter inside the Great Pyramid. Student discounts available.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Hire a licensed guide to avoid scams. Camel rides are negotiable (~$10-20). Bring water, sunscreen, and hat - it gets very hot. Photography inside pyramid forbidden.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Hot and dry year-round. Summer (May-Sep): 35-40°C. Winter (Nov-Feb): 15-25°C. Almost no rain.

landmarks.name: Colosseum
landmarks.description: Largest ancient amphitheater ever built
landmarks.significance: Hosted gladiatorial contests. Icon of Imperial Rome.
didYouKnow: The Colosseum could hold 50,000-80,000 spectators and had 80 entrance arches for crowd management
It had a retractable awning (velarium) operated by sailors to provide shade for spectators
The floor could be flooded for mock naval battles called 'naumachiae' - complete with ships and sea creatures
Wild animals were kept in underground chambers and lifted to the arena via a complex system of lifts and pulleys
The name 'Colosseum' likely comes from the colossal statue of Nero that once stood nearby, not the building's size

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Early morning (8:30 AM opening) or late afternoon. Book skip-the-line tickets online weeks in advance.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: €18 ($19 USD) includes Roman Forum and Palatine Hill. Valid 24 hours. EU citizens under 25 get discounts. Free first Sunday of month (very crowded).
explorerNotes.travelTip: Underground & arena floor tours require separate tickets but are worth it. Bring water (refill at fountains). Wear comfortable shoes - lots of walking on ancient stones. Metro Line B to 'Colosseo' station.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Hot summers (25-35°C, very crowded). Pleasant spring/fall (15-25°C, best time). Mild winters (5-15°C, fewer tourists).

landmarks.name: Eiffel Tower
landmarks.description: Wrought-iron lattice tower on Champ de Mars
landmarks.significance: Built for 1889 World's Fair. Most-visited paid monument.
didYouKnow: The Eiffel Tower was only meant to stand for 20 years as a temporary structure for the 1889 World's Fair
Gustave Eiffel had a secret apartment at the top where he entertained famous guests like Thomas Edison
The tower 'grows' about 15cm taller in summer due to thermal expansion of the iron
It was the tallest structure in the world for 41 years until the Chrysler Building in 1930
The tower is repainted every 7 years - it takes 60 tons of paint and 18 months to complete

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Early morning (9 AM) or evening for sunset/lights. Book tickets online 60 days in advance. Avoid weekends and July-August peak season.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: €29.40 ($32) for elevator to summit. €23.20 ($25) to 2nd floor. Stairs to 2nd floor: €11.80 ($13) - good workout!
localCuisine: Champagne at the tower's champagne bar (top floor)
explorerNotes.travelTip: Skip-the-line tickets sell out fast - book online! Lights sparkle for 5 minutes every hour after sunset (magical!). Pickpockets are common - watch belongings. Metro Line 6 to Bir-Hakeim or Line 9 to Trocadéro.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Mild year-round. Spring (15-20°C, beautiful). Summer (20-30°C, crowded). Fall (10-20°C, pleasant). Winter (0-10°C, romantic with lights).

landmarks.name: Great Wall of China
landmarks.description: Series of fortifications across northern China
landmarks.significance: Built to protect Chinese states. Total length: 21,196 km.
didYouKnow: The Great Wall is actually made up of many walls built over 2,000+ years, not one continuous structure
At 21,196 km long, if you walked 8 hours a day, it would take about 18 months to walk the entire length
Contrary to popular myth, the Great Wall is NOT visible from space with the naked eye
Sticky rice mortar was used in the construction - the same material used to make rice cakes!
Over 1 million workers died during construction, and many were buried within the wall itself

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Spring (April-May) or Fall (September-October) for mild weather and fewer crowds. Avoid summer heat and winter cold.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: ¥40-60 ($6-9 USD) depending on section. Mutianyu and Badaling most popular. Jiankou for adventurous hikers (wild, unrestored).
explorerNotes.travelTip: Visit Mutianyu for cable car and fewer crowds (better than touristy Badaling). Wear comfortable hiking shoes - steps are steep and uneven. Bring water. Book tours from Beijing (1.5-2 hour drive). Watch for aggressive vendors.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Hot, humid summers (25-35°C). Cold winters (-5 to 5°C, snowy). Spring/fall ideal (10-25°C). Check air quality before visiting.

landmarks.name: Machu Picchu
landmarks.description: 15th-century Inca citadel in the Andes
landmarks.significance: Most familiar icon of Inca civilization. UNESCO World Heritage.
didYouKnow: Machu Picchu was never discovered by Spanish conquistadors - it remained hidden until 1911
The stones fit so perfectly together (without mortar) that you can't fit a knife blade between them
It's built on two fault lines but has survived earthquakes for 600+ years due to advanced engineering
The site contains over 150 buildings, 100 staircases, and sophisticated water fountains that still work
Llamas are the 'gardeners' - they keep the grass trimmed by grazing throughout the ruins

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Dry season (May-September). June-August busiest. April-May or September-October for fewer crowds. Avoid rainy season (November-March).
explorerNotes.entranceFee: S/152 ($40 USD). Book online weeks/months in advance - limited to 2,500 visitors per day! Additional ticket needed for Huayna Picchu or Machu Picchu Mountain hikes.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Acclimatize in Cusco 2-3 days before visiting (altitude sickness risk). Train from Cusco or hike Inca Trail (4 days, permit required). Arrive at 6 AM opening for magical sunrise with fewer people. No food/large bags inside. Hire a guide - they reveal hidden details.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Mild year-round (10-20°C). Dry season sunny mornings, cloudy afternoons. Rainy season: expect afternoon showers. Bring layers - temperature swings common.

landmarks.name: Statue of Liberty
landmarks.description: Colossal neoclassical sculpture on Liberty Island
landmarks.significance: Gift from France. Symbol of freedom and democracy.
didYouKnow: The Statue of Liberty was a gift from France to celebrate American independence and friendship between the two nations
Lady Liberty's full name is 'Liberty Enlightening the World' and she holds a torch and a tablet dated July 4, 1776
Her copper skin is only 2.4mm thick (about the thickness of two pennies) and has turned green due to oxidation
The statue was shipped to America in 350 pieces packed in 214 crates and took 4 months to reassemble
There are 7 spikes on her crown representing the 7 continents and 7 seas of the world

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Early morning ferries (8:30-9 AM) or weekdays. Summer (June-August) extremely crowded. Spring/fall ideal.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: Free ferry access ($25 round trip). Crown tickets ($24.30) sell out months ahead - book ASAP! Pedestal access included with ferry.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Book crown tickets 3-6 months early (limited spots). Ferry leaves from Battery Park (Manhattan) or Liberty State Park (NJ). Bring ID - security like airport. Crown climb = 377 steps, no elevator. No bags allowed in crown. Get there early to beat crowds.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Hot humid summers (25-35°C). Cold winters (-5 to 5°C, windy on island). Pleasant spring/fall (10-20°C). Island is windier than mainland - bring jacket.

landmarks.name: Angkor Wat
landmarks.description: Largest religious monument in the world
landmarks.significance: Masterpiece of Khmer architecture. Symbol of Cambodia.
didYouKnow: Angkor Wat is the largest religious monument in the world, covering 162.6 hectares (400 acres)
It was built as a Hindu temple for god Vishnu, but gradually transformed into a Buddhist temple by the end of the 12th century
The temple aligns with the sun during spring equinox - the sun rises directly over the central tower
Over 300,000 workers and 6,000 elephants were used to construct the temple complex
The moat surrounding Angkor Wat is 200m wide - wider than a football field!

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Dry season (November-March). Sunrise visits magical but crowded. Visit 9 AM-3 PM for fewer people. Avoid April-May (hottest months).
explorerNotes.entranceFee: $37 USD (1-day pass). $62 (3-day pass). $72 (7-day pass). Buy at official ticket center. Passes include all Angkor temples.
nearbyAttractions: Ta Prohm (tree temple from Tomb Raider)
explorerNotes.travelTip: Hire tuk-tuk driver for full day ($15-20) - temples spread out. Dress modestly (cover shoulders/knees). Bring water, sunscreen, hat - gets extremely hot. Watch sunrise from pond behind temple for reflection shot. Download offline map - limited internet.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Hot year-round (25-35°C). Dry season (Nov-Mar) best. Rainy season (May-Oct) afternoon showers, lush greenery. Extremely humid - hydrate constantly.

landmarks.name: Petra
landmarks.description: Archaeological city famous for rock-cut architecture
landmarks.significance: Ancient Nabataean capital. One of New 7 Wonders.
didYouKnow: Petra means 'rock' in Greek - the entire city is carved directly into rose-red sandstone cliffs
The Treasury (Al-Khazneh) was actually a royal tomb, not a treasury - the name comes from a legend about hidden treasure
Petra was 'lost' to the Western world for over 500 years until Swiss explorer Johann Burckhardt rediscovered it in 1812
The Siq (entrance canyon) is 1.2 km long and at some points only 3 meters wide with walls 80 meters high
Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade was filmed here - the Treasury is the temple entrance in the movie

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Spring (March-May) or Fall (September-November). Avoid summer heat (40°C+). Early morning or late afternoon for best light on Treasury.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: JOD 50 ($70 USD) for 1-day. JOD 55 ($77) for 2-day. JOD 60 ($85) for 3-day. Free for children under 12. Jordan Pass (includes visa) is better deal.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Wear comfortable hiking shoes - lots of walking on uneven terrain. Bring plenty of water (expensive inside). Donkey/camel rides available but negotiate price first. Hike to Monastery for fewer crowds. Stay until closing (6 PM) - most tour groups leave by 3 PM. Petra by Night show (Mon/Wed/Thu) magical but touristy.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Hot, dry summers (30-40°C). Mild winters (5-15°C, can be cold at night). Spring/fall perfect (20-30°C). Minimal rainfall. Desert climate - huge temperature swings day to night.

landmarks.name: Christ the Redeemer
landmarks.description: Art Deco statue of Jesus on Corcovado mountain
landmarks.significance: Iconic symbol of Christianity and Brazil. One of New 7 Wonders.
didYouKnow: Christ the Redeemer is struck by lightning about 6 times per year - the statue has lightning rods in the fingertips
The statue's arm span (28 meters) is almost as wide as it is tall (30 meters excluding pedestal)
It's made of reinforced concrete and covered with 6 million tiny soapstone tiles
The statue weighs 635 metric tons - equivalent to about 90 elephants!
A Chapel is located at the base of the statue where weddings and baptisms are held

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Early morning (8-9 AM) for clearer skies and fewer crowds. Weekdays better than weekends. Dry season (May-October) less cloudy.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: R$83 ($17 USD) includes van transport to top. Train option: R$97 ($20). Buy tickets online to skip lines.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Weather changes quickly - morning usually clearer. Check webcam before going (statue often in clouds). Van ride steep and winding (may cause motion sickness). Book tickets online - long lines at site. Wear comfortable shoes (lots of steps). Security tight - no large bags. Best photos from Parque Lage looking up.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Warm year-round (20-30°C). Rainy season (Dec-Mar) afternoon showers. Dry season (May-Oct) less humid. Mountain top 5-10°C cooler than city, often cloudy/foggy.

landmarks.name: Stonehenge
landmarks.description: Prehistoric monument of standing stones
landmarks.significance: One of most famous prehistoric sites. Possible astronomical observatory.
didYouKnow: Each of the massive stones weighs around 25 tons - equivalent to 4 elephants - and some were transported from Wales, 240 km away!
Scientists still debate how it was built, but recent theories suggest the stones were moved using greased wooden sledges and rollers
The site aligns with the sunrise on the summer solstice and sunset on the winter solstice - suggesting it was an astronomical calendar
Stonehenge is older than the Egyptian pyramids - construction began around 3000 BC, nearly 5,000 years ago
There are over 350 burial mounds surrounding Stonehenge, making it a massive ancient cemetery and ritual site

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Early morning or late afternoon for fewer crowds. Book summer/winter solstice tickets months in advance (special access). Spring/fall pleasant weather.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: £22.50 ($28 USD) adults. £13.50 kids. Book online (required) - no walk-ins. Includes audio guide. English Heritage members free.
nearbyAttractions: Avebury Stone Circle (30 km, larger but less famous)
explorerNotes.travelTip: You can't walk among the stones (roped off 10m away) except on special solstice days or private access tours ($60+). Visitor center 2.5 km from stones - shuttle bus included. Very exposed site - bring jacket even in summer (windy!). Best photos from A303 road viewpoint (free, no ticket needed). Train to Salisbury, then bus/taxi.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Mild, rainy year-round. Summer (15-25°C, best). Winter (0-10°C, muddy). Spring/fall (10-20°C). Always windy - dress in layers.

landmarks.name: Acropolis of Athens
landmarks.description: Ancient citadel containing the Parthenon
landmarks.significance: Symbol of Ancient Greece and Western civilization.
didYouKnow: The Parthenon was built without any straight lines - all columns lean slightly inward and floors curve upward to create optical illusions of perfection
It survived nearly intact for 2,100 years until 1687 when Venetians bombarded it (Ottomans were using it as ammunition storage)
The word 'Acropolis' means 'high city' - it sits 150m above Athens on a limestone hill
The Parthenon originally had a 12-meter-tall gold and ivory statue of Athena inside, worth millions today
The marble appears white now, but it was originally painted in vibrant colors - red, blue, and gold

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Early morning (8 AM opening) or late afternoon. Avoid midday heat (no shade). October-April fewer crowds. Skip August (extreme heat, very crowded).
explorerNotes.entranceFee: €20 ($22 USD) or €30 combo ticket (7 sites including Ancient Agora). Free Nov-Mar first Sunday. EU students free. Buy online to skip lines.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Wear sturdy shoes - marble is slippery! Bring water, hat, sunscreen (brutal sun, no shade). Go EARLY to beat heat and cruise ship crowds. Slippery marble floors dangerous when wet. Metro to Acropoli station. Acropolis Museum a MUST - has original sculptures. Skip overpriced cafes at top - eat in Plaka below.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Hot, dry summers (30-40°C). Mild winters (5-15°C). Pleasant spring/fall (20-30°C). Almost no rain summer. Acropolis exposed - no escape from sun.

landmarks.name: Sydney Opera House
landmarks.description: Multi-venue performing arts center with sail-like design
landmarks.significance: Masterpiece of 20th-century architecture. Icon of Australia.
didYouKnow: The iconic 'shells' are covered with 1,056,006 Swedish-made tiles that are self-cleaning!
It took 14 years to build (1959-1973) and went 1,357% over budget - from $7 million estimate to $102 million final cost
The architect, Jørn Utzon, resigned in 1966 and never returned to see his finished masterpiece
Over 1,500 performances are held each year - that's more than 4 shows per day!
The Concert Hall's ceiling is painted in 'utzon white' and has acoustic panels that can be raised or lowered for different performances

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Early morning for photos (fewer people). Sunset for golden light. Book opera/concert tickets months ahead. Tours available daily.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: Tours: AUD $43 ($28 USD) for 1-hour guided tour. Free to walk around outside and eat at restaurants. Show tickets AUD $50-350 depending on performance.
nearbyAttractions: Sydney Harbour Bridge (500m, climb tours available)
localCuisine: Fish and chips by the harbor
explorerNotes.travelTip: Best photos from Mrs Macquarie's Chair viewpoint (1.5 km walk). Bennelong restaurant inside Opera House is expensive but special experience. Free outdoor concerts sometimes. Take harbor ferry for waterfront views ($8). Dress code for performances - check ahead. Book backstage tours for behind-the-scenes access. Train/ferry to Circular Quay.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Warm year-round. Summer (Dec-Feb): 20-30°C, humid. Winter (Jun-Aug): 10-20°C, mild. Spring/fall perfect (15-25°C). Rarely extreme. Harbor breezy - bring light jacket.

landmarks.name: Burj Khalifa
landmarks.description: Tallest building in the world
landmarks.significance: Engineering marvel. Symbol of modern Dubai.
didYouKnow: At 828 meters (2,717 feet), Burj Khalifa is the tallest structure ever built by humans - more than twice the height of the Empire State Building
It has 163 floors above ground and 2 below. The elevator ride to the top takes only 60 seconds at 10 meters per second!
The building's Y-shaped design is inspired by a desert flower and helps reduce wind forces
Over 26,000 glass panels cover the exterior - if laid out flat, they'd cover 17 soccer fields
The spire can be seen from 95 km away. During Ramadan, those at the top break their fast 2 minutes later than those on the ground due to seeing the sunset later!

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Sunset time for day-to-night views (book 'At The Top Sky' level 148). Early morning clearest visibility. Weekdays less crowded. Avoid summer daytime (extreme heat outside).
explorerNotes.entranceFee: AED 154 ($42) for Level 124+125. AED 378 ($103) for Level 148 (higher, less crowded). AED 769 ($209) for VIP prime time sunset. Book online weeks ahead - walk-in prices much higher.
nearbyAttractions: Dubai Fountain show (free, spectacular, every 30 min)
explorerNotes.travelTip: Book tickets online at least 2 weeks early (50% cheaper than walk-in). Sunset tickets most expensive but worth it. Dubai Fountain show at 6 PM and 6:30 PM free from ground level. Dress modestly (shoulders/knees covered). Metro to Burj Khalifa/Dubai Mall station. Visit during Dubai Shopping Festival (Jan-Feb) for deals. Skip At.mosphere restaurant unless celebrating - very expensive.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Extremely hot summers (40-50°C May-Sep, unbearable outdoors). Pleasant winters (15-25°C Nov-Mar, perfect). Inside always air-conditioned. Observation deck enclosed - weather doesn't matter for visit.

landmarks.name: Sagrada Família
landmarks.description: Unfinished Catholic basilica by Antoni Gaudí
landmarks.significance: Gaudí's masterpiece. UNESCO World Heritage.
didYouKnow: After 140+ years of construction, it's STILL not finished - expected completion is 2026 (the centenary of Gaudí's death)
When completed, it will have 18 towers - 12 for the apostles, 4 for the evangelists, 1 for Mary, and 1 for Jesus (tallest at 172.5m)
Gaudí spent 40 years on this project and is buried in the crypt. He said 'My client is not in a hurry' (referring to God)
The facades tell different stories: Nativity (birth of Jesus), Passion (death), and Glory (path to God) - each in completely different styles
No two columns are alike - Gaudí designed them to look like a forest, branching out to support the ceiling like trees

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Early morning (9 AM opening) for morning light through stained glass. Book weeks/months ahead - sells out daily. Weekdays less crowded than weekends.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: €26 ($28) basic entry. €36 ($39) with audio guide. €40 ($43) with tower access. Book online - walk-ins sold out. Students/seniors discounts available.
nearbyAttractions: Park Güell (2.5 km, another Gaudí masterpiece)
explorerNotes.travelTip: Book online at least 2-3 weeks early (often sold out). Time your visit for morning (9-11 AM) when sun lights up stained glass beautifully. Nativity facade gets morning sun, Passion facade afternoon. Tower tickets worth it for views. Dress code: cover shoulders/knees (they enforce it!). Metro L2/L5 to Sagrada Família. Download audio guide app. Allow 2-3 hours minimum.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Hot summers (25-35°C). Mild winters (5-15°C). Pleasant spring/fall (15-25°C). Inside climate controlled. Bring water - Barcelona is hot May-September.

landmarks.name: Hagia Sophia
landmarks.description: Former cathedral, later mosque, now mosque again
landmarks.significance: Architectural marvel of Byzantine engineering.
didYouKnow: Hagia Sophia has been a Greek Orthodox cathedral, Roman Catholic cathedral, Ottoman mosque, secular museum, and is now a mosque again (as of 2020)
The dome was the largest in the world for nearly 1,000 years and appears to 'float' due to 40 windows around its base creating a ring of light
Massive marble columns were transported from ancient temples across the Roman Empire - some from Egypt, Lebanon, and Ephesus
The building has survived dozens of earthquakes over 1,500 years thanks to innovative engineering with lead-filled joints allowing flexibility
Viking runes carved by Varangian guards in the 9th century can still be seen on the marble railings on the upper floor

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Early morning (9 AM opening) or late afternoon. Avoid midday crowds. Friday afternoons very crowded (prayer day). Spring/fall ideal weather.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: FREE (it's a functioning mosque now). Donations welcome. Used to charge when it was a museum. Respectful behavior required.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Dress modestly (shoulders/knees covered). Women must wear headscarf inside - free loaners at entrance. Remove shoes (bring socks!). Free but expect donation pressure. Visit upper gallery for mosaics (stairs, no elevator). Go early to avoid tour groups. Combine with Blue Mosque nearby. Photography allowed but respectful. Tram T1 to Sultanahmet. Prayer times may restrict access briefly.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Hot, humid summers (25-35°C). Cool winters (0-10°C). Pleasant spring/fall (15-25°C). Inside not climate controlled - can be hot in summer.

landmarks.name: Mount Rushmore
landmarks.description: Sculpture of four US presidents carved into granite
landmarks.significance: National memorial representing 150 years of American history.
didYouKnow: The faces are 60 feet (18m) tall - each eye is 11 feet wide and noses are 20 feet long!
Originally, the presidents were to be carved from head to waist, but funding ran out
No one died during the 14-year construction despite the dangerous work with dynamite and jackhammers
90% of the mountain was carved using dynamite - workers had to be within inches of the final surface
A hidden 'Hall of Records' chamber was carved behind Lincoln's head containing important US documents

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Summer (May-September) for full access and evening lighting ceremony. Fall for fewer crowds and beautiful colors. Winter can be very cold but less crowded.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: Free entrance! Parking: $10 (good for 1 year). America the Beautiful Pass accepted.
nearbyAttractions: Crazy Horse Memorial (17 miles, still being carved)
explorerNotes.travelTip: Arrive early (before 10 AM) to avoid crowds and get parking. Stay for evening lighting ceremony (9 PM summer) - very moving. Presidential Trail (0.6 mi loop) gets you closest to faces. Bring layers - weather changes quickly. Download the app for self-guided tour. Visit nearby Crazy Horse for comparison. Black Hills area needs 2-3 days to explore properly.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Summer (15-30°C, warm days, cool nights). Spring/fall (5-20°C, unpredictable). Winter (-15 to 5°C, snow, ice). High altitude - cooler than expected. Afternoon thunderstorms common summer. Always bring jacket.

landmarks.name: Notre-Dame de Paris
landmarks.description: Medieval Catholic cathedral on Île de la Cité
landmarks.significance: Masterpiece of Gothic architecture. Under restoration after 2019 fire.
didYouKnow: The 2019 fire destroyed the iconic spire and roof, but the stone vaulted ceiling saved the interior from total destruction
The cathedral's bells are named - the largest, Emmanuel, weighs 13 tons and has rung for major French events since 1681
It took 182 years to build (1163-1345) - multiple generations of craftsmen worked on it
The cathedral can hold 6,000 people and features 113 stained glass windows depicting biblical stories
Victor Hugo's 1831 novel 'The Hunchback of Notre-Dame' saved the cathedral from demolition and sparked restoration efforts

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Currently under restoration (reopening December 2024). When open: Early morning (8-9 AM) or late afternoon. Avoid weekends. Spring (April-May) and fall (September-October) best weather.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: Cathedral: Free. Tower climb: €10 ($11 USD). Treasury: €5. Book tower tickets online weeks in advance.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Check restoration status before visiting. Best viewed from Square Jean XXIII garden behind cathedral. Climb towers for gargoyle views and bells (387 steps, no elevator). Free organ concerts Sunday evenings. Metro: Cité station. Arrive early - lines get very long. Modest dress required (shoulders/knees covered). Archaeological crypt under square shows Roman Paris ruins.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (10-18°C, pleasant, some rain). Summer (20-25°C, warm, peak tourists). Fall (10-18°C, beautiful, less crowded). Winter (3-8°C, cold, festive). Rain possible year-round - bring umbrella.

landmarks.name: Chichen Itza
landmarks.description: Pre-Columbian Maya archaeological site
landmarks.significance: One of New 7 Wonders. Features astronomical precision.
didYouKnow: At spring/fall equinox, sunlight creates a shadow of a serpent descending the pyramid stairs - ancient astronomical precision!
Clapping at the pyramid base produces an echo that sounds like the sacred Quetzal bird's chirp
The pyramid has 365 steps total (91 per side × 4 + 1 platform) - one for each day of the year
A cenote (sinkhole) nearby was used for human sacrifices - archaeologists found 200+ skeletons and treasures
Climbing the pyramid has been banned since 2006 after a tourist fell to her death

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: November-April (dry season). Arrive at 8 AM opening to avoid heat and crowds. Avoid spring equinox (March 20-21) unless you want massive crowds. Summer very hot and humid.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: MXN 614 ($35 USD) - includes Chichen Itza + state tax. Cash preferred. Guide services: MXN 800-1,200 ($45-70 USD) for group.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Extremely hot - bring 2L+ water, hat, sunscreen. Wear light, breathable clothes. Vendors very persistent - polite 'no gracias' works. Stay 3-4 hours minimum. Light show at night (extra fee) is spectacular. Hire official guide at entrance for context. Bring small bills for bathrooms (MXN 5-10). Best base: Valladolid (charming town) vs Cancun (far). Sound and light show worth it.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Hot year-round (25-35°C). Dry season (Nov-Apr) best. Rainy season (May-Oct) - afternoon storms, humid. Peak heat March-May (35-40°C). Always humid. Start early to avoid midday heat.

landmarks.name: Neuschwanstein Castle
landmarks.description: 19th-century Romanesque Revival palace
landmarks.significance: Inspired Disney's Sleeping Beauty Castle.
didYouKnow: King Ludwig II only lived in the castle for 172 days before his mysterious death in 1886
Walt Disney used Neuschwanstein as inspiration for Sleeping Beauty's castle - it's the ultimate 'Disney castle'
It was built as a retreat, not a fortress - Ludwig wanted to escape reality and live in a romantic fantasy
The castle has central heating, running water, and automatic flushing toilets - cutting-edge 1880s technology!
Ludwig bankrupted Bavaria building this and other fantasy castles - he was declared insane and deposed

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: May-June or September-October (mild weather, fewer crowds). Winter magical but cold. Summer very crowded (book months ahead). Avoid August.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: €15 ($16 USD) adults. Must book online in advance (sells out weeks ahead). Combination ticket with Hohenschwangau castle: €27.
explorerNotes.travelTip: BOOK ONLINE WEEKS IN ADVANCE - sells out fast! From parking, 30-40 min uphill walk (or bus €3, horse carriage €8). Tour is only 30 min - timed entry, no photos inside. Walk 10 min further to Marienbrücke for iconic photo view. Wear good shoes - steep walk. Best photo time: morning light. Visit both castles if time allows. Stay overnight in Füssen to avoid day-trip crowds. Entry by guided tour only - 35 min. Pick up tickets 90 min before your tour time.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Summer (15-25°C, warm). Winter (-5 to 5°C, snow, magical). Spring/fall (5-15°C, unpredictable). Mountain weather changes fast - bring layers and rain jacket. Can be cold even in summer.

landmarks.name: Alhambra
landmarks.description: Palace and fortress complex with Moorish architecture
landmarks.significance: Finest example of Islamic art in Europe.
didYouKnow: The name 'Alhambra' means 'The Red One' in Arabic, named after the reddish color of the fortress walls
It contains over 10,000 intricate Arabic inscriptions in its walls - poetry, Quranic verses, and dedications
The Nasrid Palaces use no figurative art (Islamic law) - only geometric patterns, calligraphy, and nature motifs
Water features throughout use gravity alone - no pumps! An ingenious 8km aqueduct system from the mountains
Washington Irving lived here in 1829 and wrote 'Tales of the Alhambra', sparking worldwide fascination

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: March-May or September-November (mild, flowers blooming). Early morning (first entry 8:30 AM) best for photos and fewer crowds. Avoid July-August heat.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: €19.09 ($21 USD) general admission. MUST book online weeks/months in advance (6,600 daily visitor limit). Night visits available (€12).
nearbyAttractions: Mirador de San Nicolás (sunset views of Alhambra)
localCuisine: Moroccan tea and pastries in Albaicín
explorerNotes.travelTip: BOOK TICKETS 2-3 MONTHS IN ADVANCE! Nasrid Palaces entry is timed - don't miss your slot (no refunds). Arrive 30 min early. Wear comfortable shoes - lots of walking. Allow 3-4 hours minimum. Download audio guide or book guided tour. Best photos: morning light on Nasrid Palaces. Free tapas culture in Granada - eat like a local! Stay in Albaicín for authentic experience. Sunset from San Nicolás viewpoint is magical. Bring water - limited fountains.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (12-22°C, perfect). Summer (20-35°C, very hot, dry). Fall (12-24°C, pleasant). Winter (3-15°C, cold nights). High altitude - cooler than expected. Sun intense - sunscreen essential.

landmarks.name: Forbidden City
landmarks.description: Chinese imperial palace complex, 980 buildings
landmarks.significance: Largest ancient palatial structure. Home to 24 emperors.
didYouKnow: No one except the emperor could enter or leave without permission - hence 'Forbidden' City. Common people were executed for trespassing!
It has 9,999 rooms (only heaven has 10,000, so emperors took one less to show respect)
The entire complex was built without using a single nail - traditional Chinese wooden architecture
24 emperors lived here during Ming and Qing dynasties (1420-1912) - nearly 500 years of imperial rule
The palace was designed to align with cosmic harmony - even the number of door studs has meaning (9 rows of 9 = 81, imperial number)

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: April-May or September-October (mild weather). Avoid Chinese holidays (Golden Week Oct 1-7, Chinese New Year). Early morning (8:30 AM opening) essential - gets very crowded. Winter less crowded but cold.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: ¥60 ($8.50 USD) peak season (Apr-Oct), ¥40 off-season. Additional fees for Clock Museum, Treasure Gallery. Book online to skip queues.
nearbyAttractions: Jingshan Park (north, best photo view of Forbidden City)
explorerNotes.travelTip: HUGE complex - allow 3-4 hours minimum. Enter from south (Meridian Gate), exit north. Audio guide essential for context (¥40). Wear comfortable shoes - lots of walking. Jingshan Park north of palace offers best aerial photos. Very crowded - arrive at opening. Download offline map. Most buildings closed inside - viewing from courtyards. Bring passport - required for tickets. Beijing smog can obscure photos - check air quality.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (10-20°C, pleasant). Summer (25-35°C, hot, humid, rainy). Fall (10-20°C, best season). Winter (-10 to 5°C, cold, dry, clear skies). Pollution varies - check AQI.

landmarks.name: Palace of Versailles
landmarks.description: Royal château, former principal royal residence
landmarks.significance: Symbol of absolute monarchy. Hall of Mirrors is iconic.
didYouKnow: Louis XIV moved the entire French court here (20,000 people!) to keep nobles under his watch and away from Paris rebellions
The Hall of Mirrors has 357 mirrors - extremely expensive in the 1600s, showing ultimate wealth and power
It costs €15 million ($16.5M) annually just to maintain the gardens with 200,000 trees and 210,000 flowers
Marie Antoinette had a fake rustic village built where she could 'play peasant' and escape court life
The Treaty of Versailles ending WWI was signed in the Hall of Mirrors in 1919

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Tuesday-Thursday (Mon closed). Early morning (9 AM opening) or after 3 PM. April-May, September-October best weather. Avoid summer weekends. Musical Fountain Shows weekends Apr-Oct (extra fee).
explorerNotes.entranceFee: €20 ($22 USD) palace + gardens. Free first Sunday of month Nov-Mar. Audio guide €6. Book timed entry online (skip 2+ hour queues!).
nearbyAttractions: Trianon Palaces & Marie Antoinette's Estate (included in ticket)
explorerNotes.travelTip: BOOK ONLINE! Lines 2+ hours without. Full visit needs full day. RER train from Paris (€7, 40 min) - buy round-trip ticket. Start with palace, then gardens, then Trianon estates. Rent golf cart for gardens (€38/hr) or bike. Musical fountain shows magical (€10 extra). Bring picnic for gardens. Tuesday/Wednesday least crowded. Download app. Skip overpriced palace cafe. Marie Antoinette's estate often skipped but wonderful. Allow 6+ hours total.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (10-18°C, perfect for gardens). Summer (20-28°C, hot, peak crowds). Fall (10-18°C, beautiful colors). Winter (2-8°C, indoor palace visits only). Gardens best spring/summer.

landmarks.name: Golden Gate Bridge
landmarks.description: Suspension bridge spanning Golden Gate strait
landmarks.significance: Engineering masterpiece. International orange color is iconic.
didYouKnow: The 'International Orange' color was originally just primer, but it looked so good they made it permanent!
The bridge is painted continuously - by the time painters finish one end, they start over. It never ends!
11 workers died during construction (1933-37) - remarkable safety record for that era. A safety net saved 19 lives.
It sways up to 27 feet (8m) in strong winds and the roadway can rise/fall 16 feet with temperature changes
Fog horns have been sounding since 1937 - they blast every 20 seconds when fog rolls in

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: September-November (warmest, least fog). Summer very foggy! Morning usually clearer than afternoon. Weekdays less crowded than weekends.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: Free to walk/bike! Parking: $9-15. Southbound cars pay toll ($9-$10). Best views FREE from Battery Spencer, Fort Point, Crissy Field.
localCuisine: Clam chowder in sourdough bowl (Fisherman's Wharf)
explorerNotes.travelTip: Walk the bridge (1.7 mi each way, 30-45 min). East sidewalk open to pedestrians (west for bikes). DRESS WARM - always windy and cold on bridge even if SF is warm. Best photos: Battery Spencer (north side, uphill), Fort Point (underneath), Crissy Field (east). Fog is iconic but obscures views - check webcams first. Rent bike, ride to Sausalito, ferry back ($12). Sunrise/sunset beautiful. Free parking at Fort Point lot (walk up). Uber to Battery Spencer for photos, walk down to bridge.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Cool year-round (12-18°C). Summer foggy and cold on bridge (bring jacket!). Fall warmest. Always windy on bridge. Fog typically afternoon. Layers essential - SF microclimates.

landmarks.name: Leaning Tower of Pisa
landmarks.description: Freestanding bell tower famous for tilt
landmarks.significance: Leans at 3.97 degrees due to soft ground.
didYouKnow: It started leaning during construction (5 years in) because of soft clay soil - they tried to fix it by building upper floors taller on one side!
The lean increased 1mm per year for centuries. It was stabilized in 1990s and actually straightened by 45cm
Galileo allegedly dropped cannonballs from the tower to prove gravity affects all objects equally - probably a myth but great story!
It took 199 years to build (1173-1372) because of wars and financial problems - the delays actually saved it from collapse
There are 294 steps to the top - they're worn and uneven from centuries of visitors, making the climb tricky

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: April-May or September-October (mild weather, fewer crowds). Arrive early (9 AM) or late afternoon. Summer very crowded. Skip July-August.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: €20 ($22 USD) to climb tower. Book online weeks in advance (limited capacity). Cathedral free (€5 ticket for entry time). Combo tickets available.
explorerNotes.travelTip: BOOK TOWER CLIMB ONLINE! Walk-up often sold out. Timed entry - arrive 15 min early. No bags allowed (free lockers). Climb is narrow, steep, disorienting due to lean. Not for claustrophobic or young kids. Classic 'holding up tower' photos at designated spot. Piazza dei Miracoli (Square of Miracles) has all monuments - buy combo ticket. Day trip from Florence (1 hour train, €9). 3-4 hours sufficient for Pisa. Avoid overpriced restaurants near tower - walk 5 min away. Free lawn in square perfect for photos. Evening lighting beautiful, fewer crowds.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (12-20°C, pleasant). Summer (25-35°C, very hot). Fall (15-23°C, nice). Winter (5-12°C, cool, fewer tourists). Can be rainy. Summer extremely hot - bring water, sunscreen.

landmarks.name: Big Ben
landmarks.description: Clock tower at Palace of Westminster
landmarks.significance: Iconic symbol of London and UK.
didYouKnow: 'Big Ben' is actually the name of the 13.5-ton bell inside, not the tower! The tower is called Elizabeth Tower.
The clock is so accurate it's adjusted using old British pennies placed on the pendulum - adding one speeds it by 0.4 seconds per day
It has chimed every hour since 1859, except during WWI, WWII, and maintenance (2017-2022 restoration)
The clock faces are 7 meters (23 ft) in diameter - each minute hand is 4.3m long and weighs 100kg!
There's a light above the clock that illuminates when Parliament is in session - symbol of democracy at work

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Recently reopened after 5-year restoration. Tower tours limited to UK residents. Best viewed from Westminster Bridge, South Bank, or Parliament Square. Spring/summer for longer days.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: Tower tours (UK residents only): £25. Viewing from outside: FREE. Palace of Westminster tours available (book ahead): £28.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Westminster Bridge or South Bank offer best photos - Blue/golden hour stunning. Parliament Square for close-up shots. Free to view from outside anytime. Watch Changing of the Guard at Buckingham Palace (11 AM, certain days). Tube: Westminster station (exits right at Big Ben). Combine with Westminster Abbey, Churchill War Rooms. River cruise passes by. Evening floodlighting beautiful. Currently fully restored and gleaming gold/blue. Queue for Westminster Abbey - arrive early or book online.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (8-15°C, showers). Summer (15-22°C, pleasant, long days). Fall (10-15°C, crisp). Winter (2-8°C, cold, dark early). Rain possible anytime - bring umbrella. London weather unpredictable.

landmarks.name: Moscow Kremlin
landmarks.description: Fortified complex, official residence of President
landmarks.significance: Symbol of Russian power. UNESCO World Heritage.
didYouKnow: 'Kremlin' means 'fortress' in Russian - there are kremlins in many cities, but Moscow's is THE Kremlin
The red brick walls are 2.25km long, up to 6m thick, and have 20 towers - each tower is unique
It houses the world's largest bell (Tsar Bell, 200 tons) and cannon (Tsar Cannon) - both never used!
Napoleon tried to blow it up in 1812 when retreating - rain put out the fuses, saving it
The Kremlin stars atop towers are 3-3.75m wide and made of ruby glass

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: May-September (warm). Thursday museums less crowded. Arrive early. Winter magical but very cold.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: RUB 1,000 ($11 USD) grounds + cathedrals. Armoury Museum: RUB 1,000 extra. Book online.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Bring passport - required. Enter via Kutafya Tower. Security strict. Armoury timed entry - book ahead. Allow 3-4 hours. Metro: Biblioteka Lenina.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Summer (18-25°C). Winter (-10 to -5°C, snow). Layers essential.

landmarks.name: Arc de Triomphe
landmarks.description: Triumphal arch honoring those who fought for France
landmarks.significance: Center of Place Charles de Gaulle. Tomb of Unknown Soldier beneath.
didYouKnow: Napoleon commissioned it but never saw it finished - he was exiled before 1836 completion
Tomb of Unknown Soldier beneath has eternal flame - rekindled every evening at 6:30 PM since 1923
12 avenues radiate out from it - called Place de l'Étoile (Star Square)
Names of 660 French generals and 128 battles inscribed inside
De Gaulle's victory parade marched through in 1944 when Paris was liberated

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Early morning or evening for sunset. Spring/fall pleasant. Avoid July 14 (Bastille Day).
explorerNotes.entranceFee: €13 ($14 USD). Free first Sunday Nov-Mar. Climb 284 steps (no elevator).
explorerNotes.travelTip: Use underground tunnel - DON'T cross traffic circle! Climb for 360° views. Evening ceremony 6:30 PM. Metro: Charles de Gaulle-Étoile.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (10-18°C). Summer (20-25°C). Fall (10-18°C). Winter (3-8°C). Top windy - bring jacket.

landmarks.name: Pantheon
landmarks.description: Former Roman temple with unreinforced concrete dome
landmarks.significance: Best-preserved ancient Roman building.
didYouKnow: World's largest unreinforced concrete dome - still holds the record after 1,900 years!
The oculus (hole) at top is 9m wide - when it rains, it rains INSIDE (with drains)
Dome thickness decreases from base to top - ingenious Roman engineering
Raphael and Italian kings are buried here
Perfectly proportioned - dome height equals diameter (43.3m), creating perfect sphere

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Early morning (9 AM) before tour groups. Evening for light through oculus. Weekdays less crowded.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: €5 ($5.50 USD). Book online. Audio guide €6.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Visit when raining to see rain through oculus! Best light: noon. Quick visit 30-45 min. Dress code enforced. Tazza d'Oro coffee 1 min away.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (12-20°C). Summer (25-35°C, very hot). Fall (15-23°C). Winter (5-15°C). Interior always cool.

landmarks.name: Blue Mosque
landmarks.description: Historic mosque with blue interior tiles
landmarks.significance: Last great mosque of classical Ottoman architecture.
didYouKnow: Actually called Sultan Ahmed Mosque - 'Blue' from 20,000+ blue Iznik tiles inside
Has 6 minarets (very rare) - caused controversy as it matched Mecca, so 7th added to Mecca
Built in just 7 years (1609-1616) by Sultan Ahmed I when he was only 19
Architect was student of great Sinan who built Süleymaniye Mosque
One of only 3 mosques in Istanbul allowing non-Muslim visitors - FREE!

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Early morning (8:30 AM) or late afternoon between prayers. Avoid Friday noon. Spring/fall pleasant.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: FREE! Headscarf required for women (free loaners). Remove shoes.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Modest dress required. Visit between 5 daily prayers. Combine with Hagia Sophia. Best light morning. Tram: Sultanahmet stop.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (12-20°C). Summer (25-35°C, very hot). Fall (15-25°C). Winter (5-12°C, cool, rain).

landmarks.name: St. Basil's Cathedral
landmarks.description: Orthodox church with colorful onion domes
landmarks.significance: Symbol of Russia. Built by Ivan the Terrible.
didYouKnow: Legend says Ivan the Terrible blinded the architect so he could never build anything more beautiful - probably not true!
It's actually 9 separate chapels (8 around a central one) - each onion dome is unique in pattern and color
Built to celebrate Ivan's victory over the Tatars in 1552 - each chapel honors a saint from battle days
Napoleon wanted to take it back to France in 1812 - when he couldn't, he tried to blow it up (failed)
The colorful onion domes were added later - originally it was white and gold

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: May-September. Early morning or evening. Winter beautiful with snow. Avoid peak summer crowds.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: RUB 1,000 ($11 USD). Combined with Kremlin saves money. Photo outside free.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Best photos from Red Square. Interior smaller than expected - narrow stairs, low ceilings. Quick visit 30-45 min. Combine with Kremlin visit same day. Night lighting spectacular. Metro: Ploshchad Revolyutsii. Winter magical but freezing.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Summer (18-25°C). Winter (-10 to -5°C, snow). Very cold in winter - dress warm!

landmarks.name: Terracotta Army
landmarks.description: 8,000+ terracotta warriors guarding Emperor's tomb
landmarks.significance: Discovered in 1974. UNESCO World Heritage.
didYouKnow: Discovered by accident in 1974 by farmers digging a well - they initially thought they found demons!
Each warrior has unique facial features - no two are exactly alike. They're life-sized (1.8m tall, 200kg)
Originally painted in bright colors - the paint flaked off within hours of excavation when exposed to air
Estimated 8,000 warriors, 130 chariots, 670 horses - and most are still buried! Only a fraction excavated
Built for Emperor Qin Shi Huang (same guy who built Great Wall) - his tomb itself remains unopened

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: March-May or September-November. Avoid Golden Week (Oct 1-7). Weekdays less crowded. Go early (8 AM).
explorerNotes.entranceFee: CNY 150 ($21 USD) peak, CNY 120 off-season. Includes all 3 pits + museum. Guide CNY 200.
nearbyAttractions: Xi'an City Wall (old town, rent bike)
explorerNotes.travelTip: 45 km from Xi'an city - bus 306 from train station (1 hour, CNY 7). Hire guide at entrance for context - worth it! Visit Pit 1 first (most impressive), then 3, then 2. Photography allowed but no flash. Allow 2-3 hours. Avoid midday crowds. Stay in Xi'an old town (inside city wall). Muslim Quarter great for food. High-speed train from Beijing (4.5 hrs). Some original discoverer farmers sell books at gift shop.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (10-20°C, pleasant). Summer (25-35°C, very hot). Fall (15-25°C, nice). Winter (0-10°C, cold). Indoor site so weather less critical.

landmarks.name: Tower of London
landmarks.description: Historic castle housing Crown Jewels
landmarks.significance: UNESCO World Heritage. Guarded by Yeomen Warders.
didYouKnow: It's guarded by Beefeaters (Yeoman Warders) - they live there with their families and give tours
Houses the Crown Jewels including the 530-carat Cullinan I diamond - largest clear cut diamond in the world
6 ravens must live there at all times - legend says if they leave, the kingdom will fall (wings clipped!)
Was a prison for high-profile inmates - Anne Boleyn, Lady Jane Grey, Guy Fawkes all executed here
Built by William the Conqueror in 1066 - it's been royal palace, prison, armory, treasury, and zoo!

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: September-October or March-April. Weekdays better. Arrive at 9 AM opening for Crown Jewels before crowds. Summer packed.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: £33 ($40 USD) adults. Book online for discount. London Pass included. Early Tower Tour £12 extra (worth it!).
explorerNotes.travelTip: Book online - saves money and skip queues. Go straight to Crown Jewels first (before crowds). Free Beefeater tours every 30 min - very entertaining (45 min). Allow 3-4 hours minimum. White Tower (keep) has Royal Armory - amazing armor collection. Ravens usually near Wakefield Tower. Tower Bridge separate attraction. Early Tower Tour (before opening) lets you see Crown Jewels ceremony - special! Tube: Tower Hill. Combine with Tower Bridge walk.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (8-15°C). Summer (18-22°C, long days). Fall (10-15°C). Winter (3-8°C, cold). Indoor exhibits. Rain anytime - bring umbrella.

landmarks.name: Moai Statues
landmarks.description: Monolithic human figures carved by Rapa Nui people
landmarks.significance: Nearly 1,000 statues. Mystery surrounds their purpose.
didYouKnow: Nearly 1,000 moai exist on the island - 95% carved from same volcanic crater (Rano Raraku)
They represent deified ancestors and were 'walked' to their positions using ropes - not rolled!
Many have topknots (pukao) weighing 10+ tons - separate red stone from different quarry
Most face inland to watch over villages, not out to sea - only 7 face the ocean (Ahu Akivi)
The civilization collapsed around 1600s from deforestation and tribal warfare - many moai toppled during conflicts

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: October-April (summer, warmest). December-February peak season. Avoid Tapati Festival (late Jan/early Feb) unless you want crowds.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: $80 USD park pass (valid 10 days). Cash only at airport. Covers all sites. Strict rules - stay on paths!
explorerNotes.travelTip: Remote! Fly from Santiago Chile (5 hours, expensive - book early). Stay in Hanga Roa (only town). Rent car or scooter - public transport limited. Visit Ahu Tongariki at sunrise (spectacular). Rano Raraku quarry shows construction process - mind-blowing. Most sites have no facilities - bring water, sun protection. Respect moai - don't touch or climb! 3-4 days sufficient. Very expensive island - bring snacks from mainland. Stargazing incredible (dark sky reserve).
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Warm year-round (18-28°C). Summer (Dec-Mar) warmest. Winter (Jun-Aug) cooler, rainier. Subtropical - humid. Always windy. Sun intense - sunscreen essential.

landmarks.name: Borobudur
landmarks.description: Largest Buddhist temple in the world
landmarks.significance: UNESCO World Heritage. 2,672 relief panels, 504 Buddha statues.
didYouKnow: World's largest Buddhist temple - built with 2 million stone blocks without any cement or mortar!
Abandoned for centuries after a volcanic eruption and was hidden under jungle until 1814 rediscovery
It's actually a giant 3D mandala - pilgrims walk 5km through 9 levels to reach enlightenment at top
Contains 504 Buddha statues and 2,672 relief panels telling Buddha's life story
Built around 800 AD (same time as Angkor Wat) - constructed in just 75 years!

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: April-October (dry season). Sunrise visit magical but expensive. Go 6 AM for light and fewer crowds. Avoid weekends and Indonesian holidays.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: IDR 400,000 ($25 USD) foreigners. Sunrise tour IDR 650,000 ($40 USD, 4:30 AM). Book sunrise ahead.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Base in Yogyakarta (1 hour away). Sunrise tour worth extra cost - magical light, fewer crowds, access to top. Dress modestly (long pants, covered shoulders). Wear good shoes - lots of stairs. Very hot/humid - bring water. Hire guide for context. Allow 2-3 hours. Combine with Prambanan same day. Touch Buddha's hand in stupas for good luck (local belief). Volcanic ash sometimes affects visibility - check conditions. Best photos from upper terraces at golden hour.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Hot and humid year-round (25-32°C). Dry season (Apr-Oct) best. Rainy season (Nov-Mar) afternoon storms. Always humid. Sunrise cool - bring light jacket.

landmarks.name: Pompeii
landmarks.description: Roman city preserved by Mount Vesuvius eruption
landmarks.significance: Frozen in time. Provides snapshot of Roman life.
didYouKnow: Buried under 4-6m of volcanic ash in 79 AD - the city was forgotten for 1,500 years until rediscovery in 1748
Bodies were preserved as hollow cavities in ash - plaster casts show people's final moments in vivid detail
Vesuvius eruption was so hot (300°C) that people's brains boiled and exploded - gruesome but scientifically fascinating
Ancient graffiti, bakeries, brothels, fast food shops all preserved - like a Roman time capsule
Only 2/3 of Pompeii excavated - much still buried, slowly being uncovered

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: March-May or September-November. Early morning (9 AM opening) or late afternoon. Summer unbearably hot. Avoid weekends.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: €18 ($20 USD). Combined ticket with Herculaneum €22. Free first Sunday of month. Book timed entry online.
explorerNotes.travelTip: HUGE site - allow 3-4 hours minimum. Wear good shoes - uneven cobblestones. Bring water, hat, sunscreen - NO shade. Audio guide essential for context. Enter via Porta Marina. Must-sees: Forum, amphitheater, House of Faun, brothel (lupanar), plaster casts. Combine with Herculaneum (better preserved houses). Train from Naples (30 min, €3). Stay in Naples or Sorrento. Visit Vesuvius same day if energetic. Photography allowed. Very hot in summer - start early.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (12-20°C). Summer (25-35°C, brutal heat, no shade). Fall (15-23°C). Winter (8-15°C). Summer scorching - visit early morning only.

landmarks.name: Edinburgh Castle
landmarks.description: Historic fortress atop Castle Rock
landmarks.significance: Home to Scottish Crown Jewels. Symbol of Scotland.
didYouKnow: Built on extinct volcano - Castle Rock is 350 million years old!
The One O'Clock Gun fires daily at 1 PM (except Sundays) since 1861 - originally to help ships set their clocks
Houses Scotland's Crown Jewels and the Stone of Destiny (used in coronations of British monarchs)
Has been besieged 23 times - most attacked place in Britain
Mons Meg cannon (1449) could fire 150kg cannonballs 3km - one of world's largest medieval cannons

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: May-June or September. Arrive at 9:30 AM opening. Avoid Edinburgh Festival (August) - extremely crowded. Winter less crowded, shorter days.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: £19.50 ($24 USD). Book online for discount. Audio guide included. Free for under 5s.
nearbyAttractions: Royal Mile (walk down to Holyrood Palace)
explorerNotes.travelTip: Book online - walk-up often sold out. Allow 2-3 hours. Walk up Royal Mile or take bus. Crown Jewels first (before crowds). One O'Clock Gun at 1 PM daily. St Margaret's Chapel (oldest building). Great views of Edinburgh. Military Tattoo in August (book year ahead). Combined ticket with Holyrood Palace available. Steep walk up - wear good shoes. Very windy at top - bring jacket.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (8-13°C). Summer (15-19°C). Fall (9-14°C). Winter (3-7°C, dark by 4 PM). Always windy on castle rock. Rain possible anytime.

landmarks.name: Potala Palace
landmarks.description: Former winter residence of Dalai Lamas
landmarks.significance: UNESCO World Heritage. Symbol of Tibetan Buddhism.
didYouKnow: Built at 3,700m altitude - contains over 1,000 rooms, 10,000 shrines, 200,000 statues
Former winter residence of Dalai Lamas - current Dalai Lama fled to India in 1959
The White Palace is for secular use, Red Palace for religious - 13 stories tall
Construction took 50 years and 7,000 workers - no nails used, all wooden joints
Contains tombs of 8 Dalai Lamas in golden stupas - some covered in jewels and gold

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: April-October. Book weeks ahead (400 daily visitor limit). Avoid Chinese holidays. Morning visits only. Altitude acclimatization crucial.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: CNY 200 ($28 USD) peak, CNY 100 off-season. Must book with tour agency (foreigners). Passport required. 1-hour timed visit.
explorerNotes.travelTip: MUST book through tour agency - independent travel restricted in Tibet. Lhasa altitude 3,650m - spend 2-3 days acclimatizing first. Palace visit only 1 hour (timed). No photos inside. Steep stairs - take it slow. Morning light best for photos. Respect religious site - dress modestly. Visit Jokhang Temple (more important religiously). China visa + Tibet Permit required. Best season spring/fall. Winter very cold. Controversial political situation - research before visiting.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (5-15°C). Summer (10-20°C). Fall (5-15°C). Winter (-10 to 5°C, very cold). High altitude - strong sun. Dry climate. Altitude sickness common.

landmarks.name: Mont-Saint-Michel
landmarks.description: Tidal island with medieval abbey
landmarks.significance: Becomes island at high tide. Pilgrimage site since 8th century.
didYouKnow: The island is completely surrounded by water only during highest tides - tides can rise 15m!
It inspired the castle in Disney's Tangled and influenced Minas Tirith in Lord of the Rings
The abbey at top was built starting in 708 AD - it's been pilgrimage site for 1,300 years
Only 30 people live on the island year-round - but 3 million tourists visit annually
The causeway was added in 1879 - before that, pilgrims crossed tidal sands (deadly quicksand!)

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: April-June or September-October. High tide times for island effect. Avoid summer weekends (packed). Early morning or evening magical.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: Village: Free. Abbey: €11 ($12 USD). Parking: €15 (2.5km away, shuttle €3). Free Nov-Mar first Sunday.
localCuisine: Omelette de la Mère Poulard (famous fluffy omelet)
explorerNotes.travelTip: Check tide times - high tide makes it magical island. Parking 2.5km away (shuttle or walk 30 min). Arrive early (before 10 AM) or after 5 PM to avoid crowds. Abbey at top - steep climb. Famous omelet restaurant overpriced (€30!) but iconic experience. Stay overnight on island to see after day-trippers leave. Night lighting beautiful. Wear comfortable shoes - cobblestone streets, stairs. Day trip from Paris possible (3.5 hour train + bus). Best photos: across bay at high tide, from causeway. Tour France base: Rennes or Saint-Malo.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (8-15°C, pleasant). Summer (15-22°C, crowded). Fall (10-16°C, nice). Winter (5-10°C, cold, windy). Always windy. Rain common.

landmarks.name: Milan Cathedral
landmarks.description: One of largest cathedrals in world
landmarks.significance: Took 600 years to complete. 135 spires, 3,400 statues.
didYouKnow: Took 600 years to complete (1386-1965) - Napoleon had facade finished for his 1805 coronation
Has 135 spires, 3,400 statues, and 700 figures - largest number of statues on any building in world
The roof has 3,600 statues and 135 spires you can walk among - like a Gothic forest in sky
Golden Madonna statue (La Madonnina) at top is Milan's symbol - there's a law that no building can be taller
Built entirely of pink-hued Candoglia marble - quarried 100km away, transported via special canals

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: April-May or September-October. Early morning (8 AM) or late afternoon. Avoid fashion week. Rooftop at sunset spectacular.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: €10 ($11 USD) cathedral + museum. Rooftop: €14 (stairs) or €22 (elevator). Combined tickets available. Book online.
nearbyAttractions: Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II (luxury shopping arcade)
explorerNotes.travelTip: ROOFTOP IS MUST - walk among spires and statues (magical!). Book rooftop tickets online. Dress code: shoulders/knees covered. Cathedral free but museum/rooftop cost. Stairs cheaper than elevator (worth the climb). Best photos from rooftop. Visit Galleria next door (beautiful shopping arcade). The Last Supper nearby - BOOK 3 months ahead. Morning light best for facade photos. Pigeons everywhere - be careful. Fast fashion capital - great shopping. Milan efficient metro. 2-3 days sufficient for Milan. Base for Lake Como day trip.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (12-20°C). Summer (25-32°C, hot, humid). Fall (12-20°C). Winter (0-8°C, foggy). Summer very hot - rooftop scorching.

landmarks.name: Atomium
landmarks.description: Building shaped like iron crystal magnified
landmarks.significance: Built for 1958 World's Fair. Symbol of Brussels.
didYouKnow: Represents an iron crystal magnified 165 billion times - built for 1958 World Expo
Was supposed to be temporary but became permanent symbol of Brussels
The 9 spheres are 18m diameter - connected by tubes with escalators and stairs
Top sphere has restaurant with 360° views - you can sleep there (1 night stays available!)
Recently renovated - the spheres used to be aluminum but now clad in stainless steel

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: April-September. Weekdays less crowded. Evening for night lighting. Clear days for views from top.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: €16 ($18 USD) adults. Combined tickets with Mini-Europe available.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Metro: Heysel station. Allow 1-2 hours. Top sphere has restaurant. Can book overnight stay in sphere (unique!). Views from top on clear days. Quirky and fun - very Instagrammable. Combine with Mini-Europe next door. Brussels compact - easy to visit multiple sites. Chocolate shops everywhere. Beer culture strong.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (8-15°C). Summer (15-22°C). Fall (10-15°C). Winter (2-7°C). Rain common - bring umbrella.

landmarks.name: CN Tower
landmarks.description: Communications and observation tower
landmarks.significance: Tallest free-standing structure in Western Hemisphere.
didYouKnow: Was world's tallest free-standing structure for 32 years (1975-2007) at 553m
Glass floor at 342m lets you look straight down - not for the faint of heart!
EdgeWalk lets you walk around outside rim at 356m while tethered - highest hands-free circle walk
Struck by lightning 75+ times per year - built to withstand it
360 Restaurant revolves once every 72 minutes - dining with rotating views

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: May-October. Sunset for best views. Clear days essential. Weekdays less crowded. Avoid Canadian holidays.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: CAD $40 ($30 USD) general admission. SkyPod (highest level) CAD $15 extra. EdgeWalk CAD $225. Book online.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Book online - saves time and money. Go on clear day (check weather). Sunset timing perfect for day-to-night views. Glass floor freaks people out - fun to watch! EdgeWalk requires reservation weeks ahead. 360 Restaurant reservation gets you priority elevator access. Allow 1-2 hours. Very touristy but worth it once. Toronto Pass includes admission. Combine with aquarium below. Best photos from Toronto Islands ferry. Winter very cold but beautiful city views.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (5-15°C). Summer (20-27°C, perfect). Fall (10-18°C, beautiful colors). Winter (-10 to 0°C, very cold). Indoor observation deck.

landmarks.name: Chrysler Building
landmarks.description: Art Deco skyscraper with triangular crown
landmarks.significance: Finest example of Art Deco architecture.
didYouKnow: Was world's tallest building for 11 months (1930-1931) before Empire State Building topped it
Art Deco crown has triangular windows in sunburst pattern - illuminated at night, stunning!
Built by car manufacturer Walter Chrysler - gargoyles shaped like Chrysler car hood ornaments
Spire was assembled inside and hoisted up in 90 minutes - surprise move to beat rival 40 Wall Street
Still world's tallest brick building - made with 3,826,000 bricks

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: Year-round for exterior photos. Best views at night when crown is lit. Blue hour magical.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: FREE to view exterior! Interior/observation deck NOT open to public (private offices). Lobby sometimes accessible during business hours.
explorerNotes.travelTip: Can't go inside (offices only) but exterior viewing is spectacular. Best photos from Grand Central Terminal area or Tudor City Bridge. Night lighting beautiful - crown changes colors for holidays. Lobby Art Deco details worth peek during business hours (not guaranteed access). Combine with Grand Central tour (free). Walk to Empire State Building for comparison. Midtown location - easy subway access. Best photos: early morning or dusk. Top of the Rock (Rockefeller) offers views of Chrysler Building from observation deck.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Spring (10-18°C). Summer (25-30°C, hot, humid). Fall (10-20°C, beautiful). Winter (-5 to 5°C, cold). Exterior viewing any weather.

landmarks.name: Grand Canyon
landmarks.description: Steep canyon carved by Colorado River
landmarks.significance: UNESCO World Heritage. 277 miles long.
didYouKnow: 277 miles long, up to 18 miles wide, over 1 mile deep - but the Colorado River is only 300 feet wide at bottom!
Rocks at bottom are 2 billion years old - nearly half the age of Earth itself
Temperatures can vary 25°F (14°C) from rim to canyon floor - different ecosystems at different elevations
Only 5 million years old (young geologically) - carved by Colorado River over time
Less than 1% of visitors hike to bottom - most just view from rim

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: March-May or September-November. Summer very hot at bottom, crowded at rim. Winter rim beautiful but cold, North Rim closed. South Rim open year-round.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: $35 per vehicle (7 days). America the Beautiful Pass accepted ($80/year, covers all national parks). Free shuttles within park.
explorerNotes.travelTip: South Rim vs North Rim - South Rim easier access, more facilities, open year-round. North Rim more remote, cooler, closed Nov-May. Sunrise/sunset at Mather Point or Yavapai Point magical. Rim Trail easy walk with stunning views. Bright Angel Trail if hiking down (don't hike to bottom and back same day - dangerous!). Mule rides available (book months ahead). Desert View Drive scenic. Stay in park lodges (book year ahead) or Tusayan (gateway town). Phantom Ranch at bottom (lottery for reservations). Helicopter tours expensive but spectacular. Very dry - bring water. Summer 100°F+ at bottom. Altitude 7,000ft at rim - take it easy.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Rim: Spring (5-18°C). Summer (15-30°C). Fall (5-20°C). Winter (-10 to 5°C, snow). Bottom: 10-15°C warmer. Summer extreme heat at bottom (40°C+). Very dry.

landmarks.name: Victoria Falls
landmarks.description: One of largest waterfalls in world
landmarks.significance: The Smoke That Thunders. UNESCO World Heritage.
didYouKnow: Largest waterfall in world by total area - 1,708m wide and 108m high (bigger than Niagara!)
Local name 'Mosi-oa-Tunya' means 'The Smoke That Thunders' - spray visible 30km away
During peak flow (March-May), 500 million liters per minute cascade over the edge
Devil's Pool at top (Zambia side) - natural infinity pool where you can swim at edge during low water (Sept-Dec)
The falls are on border of Zambia and Zimbabwe - you can visit from both sides (need visas)

explorerNotes.bestTimeToVisit: February-May (peak flow, massive spray). August-November (low water, better views, Devil's Pool accessible). Full moon nights for 'Lunar Rainbow'.
explorerNotes.entranceFee: Zimbabwe: $30 USD. Zambia: $20 USD. Day trip visa $50 for both sides (recommended). Devil's Pool $155 USD (includes activities).
nearbyAttractions: Devil's Pool (Zambia side, low water season)
explorerNotes.travelTip: Zimbabwe side has better views (2/3 of falls). Zambia side gets you closer (Devil's Pool). Visit both if possible (day trip visa). Peak flow (Feb-May) = massive spray, need raincoat, camera protection. You WILL get soaked! Low water (Aug-Nov) = better photos, see rock face, Devil's Pool accessible. Helicopter 'Flight of Angels' ($150, 15 min) - spectacular aerial views, worth it! Bungee jump from bridge if brave. White water rafting world-class. Stay in Victoria Falls town (Zimbabwe) or Livingstone (Zambia). Bring waterproof bag for electronics. Full moon walks available. Chobe day trip for elephants. Zimbabwe/Zambia visas required - check requirements. Yellow fever vaccination certificate needed.
explorerNotes.avgWeather: Hot year-round (20-30°C). Dry season (May-Oct). Wet season (Nov-Apr, hot and humid). Always bring rain gear near falls - constant spray.

landmarks.name: Mount Fuji
landmarks.description: Tallest peak in Japan
landmarks.significance: Sacred symbol of Japan. UNESCO World Heritage.

landmarks.name: Uluru (Ayers Rock)
landmarks.description: Large sandstone rock formation
landmarks.significance: Sacred to Aboriginal Australians. Changes color at sunset.

landmarks.name: Niagara Falls
landmarks.description: Group of three waterfalls
landmarks.significance: Highest flow rate in North America.

landmarks.name: Santorini
landmarks.description: Volcanic island with white-washed buildings
landmarks.significance: Remains of massive eruption. May have inspired Atlantis.

landmarks.name: Empire State Building
landmarks.description: 102-story Art Deco skyscraper
landmarks.significance: Cultural icon. Was world's tallest for 40 years.

landmarks.name: Westminster Abbey
landmarks.description: Gothic abbey, coronation site of monarchs
landmarks.significance: Burial site of monarchs and famous Britons.

landmarks.name: Kaaba (Mecca)
landmarks.description: Holiest site in Islam, direction of prayer
landmarks.significance: Destination of Hajj pilgrimage.

landmarks.name: Kinkaku-ji (Golden Pavilion)
landmarks.description: Zen Buddhist temple covered in gold leaf
landmarks.significance: UNESCO World Heritage. Symbol of Kyoto.

landmarks.name: The White House
landmarks.description: Official residence of US President
landmarks.significance: Symbol of US presidency. 132 rooms.

landmarks.name: Brooklyn Bridge
landmarks.description: Hybrid suspension bridge
landmarks.significance: First steel-wire suspension bridge. Icon of New York.

landmarks.name: Space Needle
landmarks.description: Observation tower with saucer-shaped top
landmarks.significance: Built for 1962 World's Fair. Symbol of Seattle.

landmarks.name: Luxor Temple
landmarks.description: Ancient Egyptian temple complex
landmarks.significance: Center of Opet Festival. Avenue of Sphinxes.

landmarks.name: Karnak Temple
landmarks.description: Vast mix of temples and buildings
landmarks.significance: Second-largest ancient religious site.

landmarks.name: Abu Simbel Temples
landmarks.description: Two massive rock-cut temples
landmarks.significance: Four colossal statues of Ramesses II. Relocated in 1960s.

landmarks.name: Temple of Heaven
landmarks.description: Imperial complex where emperors prayed for harvests
landmarks.significance: Perfect example of Ming architecture.

landmarks.name: Bran Castle
landmarks.description: Medieval castle in Transylvania
landmarks.significance: Known as 'Dracula's Castle'.

landmarks.name: Hollywood Sign
landmarks.description: Landmark sign on Mount Lee
landmarks.significance: Cultural icon representing film industry.

landmarks.name: St. Peter's Basilica
landmarks.description: Largest church building by interior area
landmarks.significance: Built over tomb of St. Peter. Michelangelo designed dome.

landmarks.name: Sistine Chapel
landmarks.description: Chapel famous for Michelangelo's ceiling
landmarks.significance: 'Creation of Adam' ceiling. Papal conclave held here.

landmarks.name: Louvre Museum
landmarks.description: World's largest art museum
landmarks.significance: Houses Mona Lisa. Glass pyramid entrance is iconic.

landmarks.name: Hermitage Museum
landmarks.description: Museum housed in Winter Palace
landmarks.significance: Second-largest art museum. 3 million items.

landmarks.name: Brandenburg Gate
landmarks.description: 18th-century neoclassical monument
landmarks.significance: Symbol of German unity. Stood by Berlin Wall.

landmarks.name: Reichstag Building
landmarks.description: German parliament with glass dome
landmarks.significance: Norman Foster's dome symbolizes transparency.

landmarks.name: Cliffs of Moher
landmarks.description: Sea cliffs stretching 14 kilometers
landmarks.significance: One of Ireland's most visited attractions.

landmarks.name: Matterhorn
landmarks.description: Mountain with near-symmetrical pyramidal peak
landmarks.significance: One of most photographed mountains. Inspired Toblerone logo.

landmarks.name: Tikal
landmarks.description: Ancient Maya city with temples in rainforest
landmarks.significance: Major Maya center. Largest excavated site in Americas.

landmarks.name: Iguazu Falls
landmarks.description: 275 individual waterfalls
landmarks.significance: Eleanor Roosevelt: 'Poor Niagara' upon seeing them.

landmarks.name: Galápagos Islands
landmarks.description: Volcanic archipelago with unique wildlife
landmarks.significance: Inspired Darwin's theory of evolution. UNESCO World Heritage.

landmarks.name: Serengeti
landmarks.description: Vast ecosystem known for wildlife migration
landmarks.significance: Greatest wildlife spectacle. UNESCO World Heritage.

landmarks.name: Amazon Rainforest
landmarks.description: Largest tropical rainforest in world
landmarks.significance: Lungs of the Earth. Biodiversity hotspot.

landmarks.name: Great Barrier Reef
landmarks.description: Largest coral reef system in world
landmarks.significance: Visible from space. UNESCO World Heritage.

landmarks.name: Northern Lights
landmarks.description: Natural light display in polar regions
landmarks.significance: Aurora borealis. Bucket list natural phenomenon.

landmarks.name: Venice Canals
landmarks.description: City of canals built on 118 islands
landmarks.significance: UNESCO World Heritage. Unique floating city.

landmarks.name: Angkor Thom
landmarks.description: Ancient walled city with Bayon temple
landmarks.significance: Last capital of Khmer Empire. Stone faces iconic.

landmarks.name: Tiananmen Square
landmarks.description: Large city square, one of largest in world
landmarks.significance: Historic center of Beijing. Site of major events.

landmarks.name: Red Square
landmarks.description: City square separating Kremlin from historic district
landmarks.significance: Heart of Moscow. St. Basil's and Lenin's Mausoleum here.

landmarks.name: Times Square
landmarks.description: Major commercial intersection with bright billboards
landmarks.significance: Crossroads of the World. New Year's Eve ball drop.

landmarks.name: Himeji Castle
landmarks.description: Hilltop Japanese castle complex
landmarks.significance: Most spectacular example of Japanese castle architecture.

landmarks.name: Dubai Marina
landmarks.description: Artificial canal city with skyscrapers
landmarks.significance: World's largest man-made marina. Symbol of modern Dubai.

landmarks.name: Spanish Steps
landmarks.description: Monumental stairway of 135 steps
landmarks.significance: One of most popular meeting places in Rome.

landmarks.name: Shibuya Crossing
landmarks.description: Busiest pedestrian crossing in world
landmarks.significance: Up to 3,000 people cross at once. Icon of Tokyo.

landmarks.name: Dubai Fountain
landmarks.description: Choreographed fountain system on Burj Khalifa Lake
landmarks.significance: World's largest choreographed fountain. 900 feet long.

landmarks.name: Windsor Castle
landmarks.description: Official residence of British monarch
landmarks.significance: Oldest and largest occupied castle in world. Still used by royals.

landmarks.name: Schönbrunn Palace
landmarks.description: Former imperial summer residence
landmarks.significance: UNESCO World Heritage. 1,441 rooms. Beautiful gardens.

landmarks.name: Alcatraz Island
landmarks.description: Former federal prison on island
landmarks.significance: Housed famous criminals. 'The Rock' is iconic.

landmarks.name: Rialto Bridge
landmarks.description: Oldest bridge across Grand Canal
landmarks.significance: Iconic Venetian landmark. Central to city's commerce.

landmarks.name: Petronas Twin Towers
landmarks.description: Twin skyscrapers, tallest twin towers in world
landmarks.significance: Icon of Malaysia. Skybridge connects towers.

landmarks.name: Marina Bay Sands
landmarks.description: Integrated resort with iconic rooftop infinity pool
landmarks.significance: Architectural marvel. Symbol of modern Singapore.

landmarks.name: Mont Blanc
landmarks.description: Highest mountain in Alps
landmarks.significance: Western Europe's highest peak. Popular mountaineering destination.

landmarks.name: Yellowstone National Park
landmarks.description: First national park, famous for geysers
landmarks.significance: Old Faithful geyser. Supervolcano beneath. UNESCO World Heritage.

landmarks.name: Yosemite National Park
landmarks.description: National park famous for granite cliffs and waterfalls
landmarks.significance: El Capitan, Half Dome. UNESCO World Heritage.

landmarks.name: Banff National Park
